business.headline: Wipro hires 888 more employees in Q1. What's driving the headcount growth?Wipro's total employee count rose by 888 during the quarter ended June 30, taking its workforce to 243,044. This follows a period in which most large IT companies either slowed hiring or trimmed headcount amid weak global technology spending.

Wipro Q1 Results: Profit rises 1%, revenue up 11%; declares Rs 2 interim dividendWipro reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 3,352 crore for the quarter ended June 30, up 1% from Rs 3,330 crore in the same period last year.

Why the Rs 200 meal voucher tax benefit won't apply to your ITR this yearAs the ITR filing season gathers pace, our 'KNOW YOUR TAXES' series explains the tax rules that matter most. In this edition, we clear up one of the biggest misconceptions doing the rounds, whether you can claim the new Rs 200 per meal voucher tax benefit while filing your ITR this year.

Markets ends flat as banking drag offsets IT gains amid US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex ended almost unchanged, rising just 1.44 points to close at 77,186.87. The NSE Nifty50 slipped 5.75 points, or 0.02%, to settle at 24,072.75.

No micromanagement, no after-hours calls: Why an Indian engineer chose a UK firmAn Indian software engineer working remotely for a UK-based company has shared why he has stayed with his employer for more than five years, saying that while being paid in pounds is a nice perk, it isn't the biggest reason.

The hidden cost of renting a house in IndiaA report says tenants across six Indian metros have Rs 1.26 lakh crore locked in security deposits with landlords. The finding highlights how upfront deposits, refund risks and idle savings are reshaping the cost of renting.

Leaked Kudankulam plant data not linked to nuke systems: Nuclear power operatorIndia's nuclear power operator rejected reports of a sensitive data breach at the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project, saying the leaked files relate only to conventional Balance of Plant facilities and not to any nuclear safety or security systems.

ITR filing alert: More than 5 crore taxpayers have checked this statement. Have you?With the ITR filing deadline nearing, taxpayers are busy gathering documents. But before you file your return, the Income Tax Department wants you to complete one simple step, one that over 5 crore taxpayers have already taken.

ICICI Lombard shares crash up to 15%: Why market investors are dumping the stockICICI Lombard reported a 46% year-on-year decline in net profit to Rs 403 crore for the quarter ended June, compared with Rs 747 crore in the corresponding period last year.

HCLTech, TechM, Infosys, TCS rise up to 3%. Why are IT stocks rising today?IT stocks rebounded in early trade on Thursday, led by HCLTech and LTTS ahead of key June-quarter earnings. Softer US inflation data supported sentiment, though Middle East tensions kept broader market gains limited.
Sensex, Nifty open higher as IT stocks lead gains; investors await Q1 earningsThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 184.38 points to 77,369.81, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 42.40 points to 24,120.90 as of 9:30 am.

EPFO credits FY26 interest to 34 crore accounts. Here's how to check your PF balanceEPFO has credited 8.25% FY26 interest to 34 crore member accounts by July 15. The early rollout, enabled by its centralised IT system, marks the fastest completion yet.

Diesel, jet fuel exports face higher tax as Brent crude stays above $85India has raised export duties on diesel and aviation turbine fuel after crude prices surged amid renewed US-Iran tensions. The move reflects supply concerns around key shipping routes and pressure from higher global oil prices.

Why Samsung and SK Hynix shares crashed up to 9% despite the AI boomSamsung Electronics and SK Hynix shares slumped as a global semiconductor sell-off spread from Wall Street to Asia. The fall reflected profit booking, valuation worries and fresh questions over the pace of AI spending despite firm chip demand.

China's economy slows to 4.3% in Q2 as domestic demand weakensOfficial data released by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) on Wednesday showed that the world's second-largest economy expanded by 4.3% between April and June, down from 5% in the first quarter.

Rs 62,500 crore push for mobile manufacturing: Will it make smartphones cheaper?The move is expected to help India strengthen its position as a global manufacturing hub and the scheme will run for five years, from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31.

India-UK FTA goes live: Why UK High Commissioner calls it the 'new gold standard'UK High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron called the India-UK FTA the "new gold standard" for free trade agreements and said it should be viewed as the beginning of a deeper partnership rather than its final destination.

Sensex recovers but profit booking wipes out most gains amid oil concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex settled 130.49 points, or 0.17%, higher at 77,185.43 after climbing more than 500 points during the session. The NSE Nifty50 also erased a large part of its intraday gains to close 27.70 points, or 0.11%, higher at 24,078.50.

Centre approves Semicon 2.0 with Rs 1.27 lakh crore outlayThe Union Cabinet has approved Semicon 2.0 with a Rs 1.27 lakh crore outlay for chip design and manufacturing. The move seeks to expand India's role in the global semiconductor supply chain and reduce import dependence.

Files linked to Kudankulam N-power plant exposed in data breach: ReutersA ransomware group has published a large cache of stolen data on the dark web, claiming it includes blueprints of parts of India's largest nuclear power plant Kudankulam. The hackers said the data was obtained from Anil Ambani's Reliance Group, a contractor at the plant.

entertainment.headline: Aamir Khan says his 3 Idiots role not based on Wangchuk amid his hunger strike Aamir Khan said Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots was not based on activist Sonam Wangchuk. His remarks revived the old debate even as concern grew over Wangchuk's 20-day hunger strike.

Kim Kardashian defends post after grandmother's death, Lewis Hamilton backs her Kim Kardashian clarified that her lakeside holiday post had been scheduled before the death of her grandmother, Mary Jo 'MJ' Shannon, after facing online backlash. Hours later, Lewis Hamilton shared a heartfelt message extending support.

The Odyssey, Ramayana and Mahabharata: The similarities you never noticed Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is being framed through parallels with the Ramayana and the Mahabharata. The comparison rests on shared themes of exile, temptation, divine intervention and homecoming, even as the epics remain culturally distinct.

Mary Jo MJ Shannon dies at 91: Kris Jenner mourns, Kim Kardashian shares tribute Kris Jenner announced that her mother, Mary Jo 'MJ' Shannon, has died at 91, prompting tributes from across the Kardashian family. Their messages remembered MJ as the family's emotional centre and a lasting influence on their values and work ethic.

Tom Cruise raves about Nolan's The Odyssey, praises brilliant cast and crew Actor Tom Cruise praised Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey after attending a late-night screening and sharing a premiere photo on social media. His post highlighted his enthusiasm for the theatrical experience ahead of the film's worldwide release.

Will give up my life if proven guilty: Raj Kundra on pornography case Businessman-turned-actor Raj Kundra has again defended himself in the ongoing pornography case and said he is ready to face punishment if found guilty. He also said the arrest led to the shutdown of his businesses and left him scarred by 63 days in Arthur Road Jail.

Vicky Kaushal wraps Katrina Kaif in a hug, shares adorable birthday wish Actor Vicky Kaushal wished actor-wife Katrina Kaif on her 43rd birthday with a celebration photo and Hindi caption, calling her 'Jaan', adding a warm personal touch to the social media post.

Paresh Rawal says Akshay Kumar's OMG 2 was his concept but he wasn't credited Actor Paresh Rawal has claimed that the story that became OMG 2 began as an idea he developed with director Amit Rai. He said the film later changed course, and he was not credited for the original concept.

Biting a bitter pill to run my house: Anjali Anand on Dhamaal 4 body-shaming jokes Content creator Soumya Dubey questioned actor Anjali Dinesh Anand over fat-shaming jokes in Dhamaal 4 and her interview remarks on body positivity. Anand replied that she is taking the roles she gets while waiting for the industry climate to change.

Christopher Nolan too tough to impress? Tom Holland, Matt Damon reveal Actors Tom Holland and Matt Damon said Christopher Nolan is far more open and collaborative on The Odyssey than his reputation suggests. The duo reacted to the assumptions about Nolan being rigid or unapproachable.

Actor Rasika Dugal says Manto pulled her out of a career slump before Mirzapur Actor Rasika Dugal looked back on how Manto steadied her during a difficult phase and how a text to Karan Anshuman led to Mirzapur. She said the roles, along with Delhi Crime, showed her range and underscored how chance shapes casting.

Varavu review: Joju George's action thriller is all noise, little substanceRelease Date:Thu Jul 16, 2026 Varavu movie review: Featuring Joju George in the lead, alongside Arjun Ashokan, Saniya Iyappan, and others, this revenge-driven action thriller seems like a mass feast at first glance but is hollow in content.

Anjali Anand says Ranveer Singh apologised when her first shoot was delayed Actor Anjali Anand recalled co-actor Ranveer Singh apologising after her first shoot on Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani was delayed. She said his sensitivity, encouragement and continued warmth stayed with her.

I can't remain silent: Sonakshi Sinha joins voices backing Sonam Wangchuk Sonakshi Sinha backed Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike in New Delhi through a strongly worded video statement. Her appeal adds to growing calls from film personalities for the government to open a dialogue.

Aamir Khan married Gauri at 60 after saying he wouldn't. His friend reveals why Amin Hajee said Aamir Khan's public hesitation about marriage reflected the stage of his relationship with Gauri Spratt. He said her need to balance the relationship with caring for her son was central to their eventual decision.

Half Man review: Richard Gadd, Jamie Bell excel in raw, toxic drama series Actor-creator Richard Gadd's Half Man follows stepbrothers Ruben and Niall through years of violence, repression and dependence. The six-part drama series directed by Alexandra Brodski and written by Gadd, examines how trauma and masculinity trap both men in a destructive bond.

99% on Rotten Tomatoes, glowing reviews: Nolan's The Odyssey lives up to the hype The first reviews for director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey have praised its scale, emotion and ensemble cast. The early response points to strong expectations for its theatrical release despite some reservations about uneven stretches and dialogue.

Why doesn't India have a true IMAX 15/70 screen? Real reasons explained As Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey releases in India, the discussion once again moves to the country's lack of true 15/70mm IMAX theatres. Why hasn't India invested in the world's highest-quality IMAX format, and what does it reveal about the economics of premium cinema?

Actor Imran Khan demands accountability in NEET row amid Sonam Wangchuk's protest Actor Imran Khan has backed students in the NEET row and endorsed calls for accountability. His post adds to support for Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike and wider exam reforms.

general news.headline: Trump claims China carried out largest compromise of 2020 US poll data in historyTrump alleged that China carried out the "largest compromise of election data in history" by illicitly acquiring 220 million US voter files and that members of the US intelligence community deliberately withheld information about the alleged breach from both the public and the President.

Sonam Wangchuk enters Day 20 of hunger strike, doctors warn of organ failureDoctors said Sonam Wangchuk has entered a critical phase of prolonged fasting, warning that his organs could be affected if his condition worsens. As the Delhi High Court ordered daily health monitoring, political leaders, public figures and legal bodies urged him to end his fast, but he remained resolute.

Why Pakistan is suddenly sweating over Houthis attack on Saudi ArabiaRenewed Houthi attacks on Saudi Arabia have heightened pressure on Pakistan's regional role. Islamabad is trying to preserve diplomacy with Iran while standing by its Saudi commitments.

Why you should arrive at theatre 30 minutes early for Nolan's The OdysseyChristopher Nolan's The Odyssey is finally arriving in theatres, and fans are already treating it as a cinematic event. From arriving at the theatre early to understanding Homer's epic before the film starts, here's everything you should keep in mind before embarking on Nolan's latest.

When a death after 58-day hunger strike shook Nehru, gave birth to AndhraFive years after Independence, Gandhian leader and activist Potti Sriramulu began a fast until death demanding a separate state for Telugu-speaking people. His death after 58 days of fast forced PM Jawaharlal Nehru to act and declare the formation of the Andhra State, the first on linguistic lines.

NEET UG 2026: Punjab's Aryan Gupta tops, Rajasthan leads top 100The NTA's NEET UG 2026 state-wise topper list placed Punjab's Aryan Gupta at AIR 1 and showed Rajasthan leading the top 100. The results also underscored strong representation by women and participation from all States and Union Territories.

CJP protest is now about one man, not Dipke but Sonam WangchukThe Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) might have been born online, but its identity is now being shaped at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar. Nineteen days into his indefinite hunger strike, Sonam Wangchuk has become much more than just a protester. For many, he is now the face, conscience and symbol of the movement.

Will Rohit Sharma retire? Lord's ODI could be his last match for IndiaThe third ODI against England at Lord's on Sunday could be Rohit Sharma's final match for India, with the national selection committee unlikely to retain the veteran opener beyond the ongoing UK tour, according to a PTI report.

Aamir Khan says his 3 Idiots role not based on Wangchuk amid his hunger strikeAamir Khan said Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots was not based on activist Sonam Wangchuk. His remarks revived the old debate even as concern grew over Wangchuk's 20-day hunger strike.

The Odyssey Movie Release and Review Live: First visuals emerge from global screeningsThe Odyssey Movie Review and Release Live Updates: The Odyssey releases in India today. Follow our live blog for critic reviews, fan reactions, box office updates, social media buzz and every major highlight from Christopher Nolan's epic.

Congress worker dies as iron rod falls on him at Rahul Gandhi's event venueThe incident occurred during the preparations for Rahul Gandhi's 'Chhatron ki Goonj' (Voice of Students) programme, scheduled to be held on Friday, July 17, at a school ground in Dehradun.

India has ended up with 700 crore litres of extra ethanol capacity. What now?India raced to build ethanol production capacity, and achieved an E20 mandate five years ahead of schedule. Now, the big question is whether India has built more production capacity than its current demand. What happens to the notional surplus capacity of approximately 700 crore litres?

Did Maruti Suzuki sell a non-E20 Grand Vitara? Know why you need to pay attentionRaipur consumer court directs Maruti Suzuki to replace a Grand Vitara after holding that a January 2023-built vehicle sold in 2024 was not E20-compatible, despite India's transition to ethanol-blended petrol.

US-Iran escalation enters 6th night: Bridge strike kills 7, Tehran launches missilesThe US military said it launched fresh strikes to "further degrade Iranian military capabilities", while Iran said it targeted American bases, including an expanded air base in Jordan that it claimed was used in a US strike on an Iranian children's cancer hospital a day earlier.

Indian-origin Nitish Kaushal, member of Jaggu Bhagwanpuria gang, arrested in USNitish Kaushal was wanted for alleged links to jailed gangster Jaggu Bhagwanpuria's crime syndicate, which the FBI said operates across borders and is involved in multiple organised criminal activities.

NEET UG results declared: 11.21 lakh qualify, toppers hail from Punjab and HaryanaOver 11 lakh aspirants secured eligibility for MBBS, BDS and AYUSH admissions. Punjab's Aryan Gupta and Haryana's Panshul Bansal topped the exam with identical scores of 715.

The Odyssey, Ramayana and Mahabharata: The similarities you never noticedChristopher Nolan's The Odyssey is being framed through parallels with the Ramayana and the Mahabharata. The comparison rests on shared themes of exile, temptation, divine intervention and homecoming, even as the epics remain culturally distinct.

Many have gone, many have come: Science minister on Isro resignation waveThe minister's remarks follow concerns over nearly 100 scientists quitting ISRO in recent months and shortly after the Department of Space tightened norms by requiring all resignation and voluntary retirement requests to be cleared at the departmental level.

Pakistan deploys 8,000 more Rangers, heavy weapons in POK amid growing unrestPakistan has deployed an additional 8,000 Rangers from Punjab province to Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (POK) and moved heavy weaponry into sensitive areas amid escalating protests, sources said. India has accused Pakistan of suppressing dissent through force and urged the international community to hold Islamabad accountable for alleged human rights violations in the region.

No rift between Kohli and Gambhir: India batting coach slams rumoursIndia batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Virat Kohli and Gautam Gambhir were in regular conversation during the second ODI in Cardiff and dismissed rumours suggesting that there was a rift in the working relationship between the two.

Man's dismissive response after being caught littering railway tracks draws flakThe video showed an elderly man standing on a railway platform next to a stationary train, eating bananas and throwing the peels onto the tracks.

Monsoon deficit at 24%, exceeds the 2002 drought year's final tallyIndia's monsoon rainfall deficit has widened to 24 per cent, already bigger than the 19 per cent full-season shortfall of the 2002 drought year. The IMD blames El Nino and a neutral Indian Ocean Dipole, and forecasts heavy rain only over east and northeast India in the coming week.

Journalist detained, phone seized over messages linked to TVK MLA poaching claimPolice said journalist Vijayan was questioned over alleged communications with prime accused Thirunavukkarasu, while his mobile phone was seized for forensic examination as part of the ongoing investigation.

SpaceX scrubs Starship Super Heavy's 13th test launch due to technical reasonsSpaceX was set to launch Starship Flight 13 from Starbase with 20 V3 Starlink satellites and a series of in-flight tests. The mission would assess satellite deployment, engine restart and heat shield upgrades for future reusable Moon and Mars flights.

sports.headline: No pressure on Rohit Sharma: Team India make stance clear as retirement buzz grows Sitanshu Kotak said India are not putting Rohit Sharma under pressure despite the noise around his ODI future. Reports on Thursday suggested that the selectors are ready to move on from Rohit and he wasn't in the plans for the ODI World Cup next year.

No rift between Kohli and Gambhir: India batting coach slams rumours India batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Virat Kohli and Gautam Gambhir were in regular conversation during the second ODI in Cardiff and dismissed rumours suggesting that there was a rift in the working relationship between the two.

2nd ODI match report: Joe Root's 99 powers England to series-levelling win vs India England vs India, 2nd ODI match report: Joe Root was left stranded on 99 not out as England beat India by four wickets in the second ODI at Sophia Gardens in Cardiff on Thursday to level the three-match series 1-1.

Trump to attend World Cup final between Argentina and Spain: White House The White House has confirmed that US President Donald Trump will attend the FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain on Sunday, with FIFA president Gianni Infantino set to join him in presenting the trophy to the winners.

Ex-India captain wants Mandhana to takeover ODI, Test side from Harmanpreet Mithali Raj has backed Smriti Mandhana to succeed Harmanpreet Kaur as India's ODI and Test captain, while calling for Shafali Varma to be groomed as the T20I skipper. The former India captain said the selectors must look beyond one ICC event and build a long-term leadership roadmap.

Yamal injury scare? Spain star trains alone before World Cup final vs Argentina Lamine Yamal and Pedro Porro trained separately as Spain resumed preparations for the World Cup final against Argentina. The federation said the move was precautionary, easing concerns before Sunday's title clash.

Will Rohit Sharma retire? Lord's ODI could be his last match for India The third ODI against England at Lord's on Sunday could be Rohit Sharma's final match for India, with the national selection committee unlikely to retain the veteran opener beyond the ongoing UK tour, according to a PTI report.

ENG vs IND: Rohit Sharma fails but Virat Kohli shines with fluent 65 in Cardiff England vs India, 2nd ODI: Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma have carried India's batting for more than a decade. But the second ODI against England in Cardiff showed the two veterans in very different touch.

Kranti Gaud felicitated after scripting history in India's Lord's Test victory Kranti Gaud was felicitated by Madhya Pradesh Sports Minister Vishwas Kailash Sarang on Thursday after playing a pivotal role in India's historic Test victory over England at Lord's.

Why is KL Rahul not playing the second ODI vs England? India reveal reason India made one change to their playing XI for the second ODI against England, with wicketkeeper-batter Ishan Kishan replacing the unwell KL Rahul after England won the toss and elected to field first.

ENG vs IND: Gurnoor Brar gets penalised by ICC after Ben Duckett spat in 1st ODIGurnoor Brar was officially warned by the ICC after throwing the ball near Ben Duckett during the first ODI in Birmingham. The sanction brought one demerit point for the Brar, who accepted responsibility for the mistake.

UK government tells FIFA to probe Argentina over Falklands banner celebrationThe UK has asked FIFA to examine Argentina players displaying a Falklands claim banner after beating England. Argentina defeated the Three Lions 2-1 in Atlanta to reach the final.

Peter Drury of backwaters: Malayalam commentary on Messi magic goes viralCelebrated Malayalam commentator Shaiju Damodaran's call amplified Lionel Messi's celebrations after Argentina beat England in the semi-final. The clip was widely shared on social media on Thursday.

Thomas Tuchel got it wrong but England's biggest problem is the noise around themThomas Tuchel's tactical retreat cost England against Argentina, but another World Cup exit exposed a deeper issue. From relentless scrutiny to former players creating needless narratives, the noise around the Three Lions may be hurting them more than they realise.

PV Sindhu stuns World No. 5 Han Yue to reach Japan Open quarterfinalsPV Sindhu beat world number five Han Yue in straight games to reach the Japan Open quarterfinals. The emphatic win left her as India's lone survivor and set up a clash with Nozomi Okuhara.

Tuchel bullish on England future despite criticism following World Cup exitThomas Tuchel has commented on his England future after the Three Lions were beaten by Argentina in the semi-final of the FIFA World Cup 2026. Tuchel's time with England came under scrutiny following the defeat.

PBKS' record broken: Steve Smith powers highest successful chase in MLCSteve Smith smashed an unbeaten 110 and Andries Gous scored 132 as Washington Freedom broke Punjab Kings' record for the highest successful run chase in T20 cricket. Freedom defeated MI New York and chased down 267 runs in the MLC eliminator on Wednesday, July 15.

World Cup needs more stories like Cape Verde. Cricket only wants India vs PakFootball just ran a 48-team World Cup and is debating 64. Cricket's next 50-over edition guarantees India-Pakistan up to three times over while asking associate nations to survive a qualifying maze even to get a foot in the door.

Messi faces Lamine Yamal in World Cup final: The story of that iconic bathtub photoFrom a vintage charity photo to a New Jersey showpiece, Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal, both iconic graduates of Barcelona's famed La Masia academy, set up the ultimate generational showdown in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final. Here's the story of that iconic bathtub photoshoot from 2007.

Watch: Messi's priceless reaction as Argentina find ENG goalkeeper's cheat sheetAfter Argentina's late win over England, Lionel Messi and his teammates examined Jordan Pickford's penalty cheat sheet in Atlanta. Argentina moved to the final with Messi pulling off two stunning assists in the second half.

Watch: Bellingham slaps Argentina star as ugly scenes erupt after England's exitJude Bellingham became embroiled in a post-match confrontation with Argentina players after England's semi-final defeat. The flashpoint saw him slap Argentina's Valentin Barco right after the Three Lions were beaten.

Lionel Messi's reaction to Jude Bellingham provocation is unmissable: Watch videoJude Bellingham received a chilling reminder from Argentina fans not to mess with Lionel Messi, as the England midfielder's spat with the football legend went viral during the semi-final of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Hope I'm never that stupid: Romero slams Gary Neville after Argentina's winFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristian Romero hit back at Gary Neville after Argentina's 2-1 World Cup semi-final win over England, saying he hopes he is never "that stupid" after the former England defender criticised Argentina's centre-back pairing before the match.

No doubt Messi is the greatest: Lionel Scaloni believes GOAT debate is now overLionel Scaloni declared Lionel Messi the greatest footballer in history after Argentina's 2-1 semi-final comeback over England. Messi's two assists sent the holders into another World Cup final and to the edge of history.

Lionel Messi dedicates England win as a special gift to the late Diego MaradonaLionel Messi dedicated Argentina's dramatic World Cup semi-final win over England to Diego Maradona, saying the late legend would be "enjoying it from up above". The captain called the comeback victory "a gift" for Argentina's 1986 World Cup hero.

Argentina didn't give people a chance to talk crap: Lionel Messi after England winFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi said Argentina denied critics the chance to "talk crap" after their 2-1 comeback win over England in the FIFA World Cup semi-final. The captain also said the reigning champions always believed they were the better football team.

Is Messi really 39? Ageless, tireless and still football's greatest conductorAt 39, Lionel Messi no longer overwhelms football with pace. He now bends matches to his rhythm, turning intelligence into Argentina's greatest weapon. England discovered it the hard way. Spain are next to try and stop football's ultimate orchestrator.

Tuchel's decisions cost us: England legends slam head coach for semi-final blunderFIFA World Cup 2026: Wayne Rooney led the criticism of Thomas Tuchel after England's 2-1 World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, saying the manager's tactical decisions "cost us" the match. Shearer, Neville, Richards and Owen also questioned England's passive approach.

Argentina celebrate England win with Falklands banner. Is it breach of FIFA rules?FIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's post-match celebrations after beating England in the World Cup semi-final took a political turn as players displayed a "Las Malvinas Son Argentinas" banner. The incident could draw FIFA scrutiny over rules banning political messages inside stadiums.

Thomas Tuchel has no regrets despite England's World Cup heartbreak vs ArgentinaThomas Tuchel defended his tactical decisions after England's heartbreaking World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, insisting he had "no regrets" despite late criticism over his substitutions as the Three Lions surrendered a one-goal lead to miss out on a place in the final.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
